ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Sep-25-2008 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Kathleen Courts for Aurora Hernandez (Trustee of the Felipe G. Ibarra Trust). Crisostomo Ibarra in propria persona.

Ms. Courts stated that the contract regarding the time share in Cabo San Lucas had been cancelled due to insufficient funds to pay for it, the inability to contact the time share management due to the lack of notice when the management changed hands, and the unsuccessful attempts to get the time share transferred into the trust. Mr. Ibarra stated that he didn't understand why they failed to maintain the time share as he was able to maintain it during the Conservatorship, and that he had turned over all information to them. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Karen Farnsworth, CSR#5162. Clerk: Rosie Noguera/ J. Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT, FEES

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT
Off Calendar Nov-13-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Called with case number 285838. John Murphy appearing for beneficiary. Katherine Courts appearing for trustee. Crisostomo Ibarra appearing as the administrator of the estate. Mr. Ibarra is objecting to trustee's fees and attorney's fees. Court agrees to hear oral argument this morning. Oral argument presented by Ms. Courts and Mr. Ibarra. Court is allowing fees beginning with Mr. Murphy's petition to bring the trust under court supervision. Accounting and distribution are approved. Trustee's fees and attorney's fees are submitted. Ms. Courts will submit an order.
In the Estate, Request for additional compensation was not notice to everybody. With no opposition to the accounting and distribution, order signed. Judge Pro Tem: Dorothy L. McMath. Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR#11722. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Mizota.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT
Off Calendar Aug-21-2006 Continued To Oct-02-06 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Called with case no. 285838. John Murray appearing for beneficiary. Ken Mitchell appearing for trustee. Alan Phillips appearing for and with administrator. Tentative ruling is Felipe Jr.'s claim is offset by any rent except for $36.37. No payment to Felipe Jr. nor any rent be charged. In the estate, the report and the accounting are not property verified. No original ending bank statement is provided. Court will surcharge both the attorney and administrator $5,000.00 each for delay in closing the estate. The extraordinary fees of $3,000.00 for sale of real property is allowed. Court will allow and not approve the accounting. Mr. Murray's fees in the amount of $1,200.00 is approved. In the truste, there is no declaration in support of the the attorney's fees. Court wants to see the breakdown of fees before they are paid. Felipe Jr.'s claim is the same as in the estate. The cemetary lots are no longer assets of the trust. The timeshare in Cabo San Lucas is not resolved. Felipe Jr. is in court. He is claiming for taking care of his father and the property. He is too late to raise the claim in taking care of his father. His claim for taking care of the property is offset by rent. Both his claim for reimbursement and the request for rent are denied. Mr. Mitchell accepts the court's tentative ruling. He is requesting the estate to pay the estate's share of the house expenses in the amount of $1,429.00. Court is ordering the estate to reimburse the trust in that amount. The trust is directed to delete the cemetary lots from the assets. Personal property issue raised by the administrator. The trustee is to file a supplemental account of assets she received and disposition of those assets by 9/1. Cash distribution from the trust is approved with revision on the amount chargable to the estate beneficiaries. Matter is continued to 10/2. Judge Pro Tem: Dorothy L. McMath. Reporter: Teena Paxton. Clerk: Ella Yip.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of Final Account And Petition For Final Distribution
Off Calendar Jun-26-2006 Continued To Jul-17-06 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Called with case number 285838. Alan Phillips appearing for administrator. John Murray appearing for beneficiary Rafaelita Ibarra. Mr. Phillips wants more time to talk to his client regarding the accounting. He will file the amended accounting and petition for final distribution by 7/17. He is asking the court to set it for hearing on 8/21. The court is continuing the status to 7/17. If the amened account and petition is filed, no appearance is required. Court will set the amended accounting for hearing on 8/21 if it is filed by 7/17. Mr. Phillips to inform counsel for the trustee of the schedule for filing. Judge Pro Tem: Dorothy L. McMath. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR 11722. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Mizota.

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (1538 Cabrillo Street, San Francisco, Ca)
Off Calendar Jun-02-2006 Written Order Received
MINI MINUTES: Called with case no. 285838. Alan Phillips appearing for and with administrator. Kenneth Mitchell appearing for and with the trustee. John Murray appearing for beneficiary Rafaelita Ibarra. Court questions the special notice to Vreeland and Son. Mr. Phillips is not aware of the Request for Special Notice. Court will allow the sale to fo forward on the condition that counsel will obtain a waiver of special notice from Vreeland and Son. Sale is for 100% interest with 50% interest each in the estate and in the trust. Sale is confirmed at $970,000.00 for 100% interest. Additional bond is not required in the estate and no bond is required in the trust. On the accounting issue, the administator has not filed an amended accounting. Both the beneficiary and the trustee has filed objections to the accounting. The trustee is objecting to the allocation and the claim for rent. Court is setting a status date on 6/26 for filing of the final accounting and petition for final distribution in both cases. Counsel to come to the court to get the same hearing date. All other petitions are off calendar. The two OSC are discharged. Counsel to prepare order on sale. Judge Pro Tem: Dorothy L. McMath. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR 11722. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Mizota.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Sale Of Real Property, If Petition For Conf. Of Sale Is On File, N.A.
Off Calendar Mar-27-2006 Continued To Apr-24-06 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Kathleen Courts appearing for trustee. Alan Phillips appearing for administrator. John Murphy appearing for beneficiary. Status report filed by Mr. Murphy. His client will move out by 3/31. Mr. Phillips said the estate has no money. Bulk of things are out of the house. The decendent's things are still in the shed. The administrator can not move it. Ms. Courts said the trustee will be receiving some funds to pay for the haul away but not for painting. The trustee will hire someone to move everything out. She will try to have it done by Friday. The realtor will get the house ready for sale. Matter is continued to 4/24. No appearance is required if property is listed for sale and status report is filed by Ms. Courts. Judge Pro Tem: Dorothy L. McMath. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR 11722. Clerk: Ella Yip.
